spec_match.rationale: Accurately traverses the live DOM utilizing tree walkers to output processing instruction target/data pairs.

interactive.rationale: Very interactive: enables parsing input dynamically, clicking tree nodes to update property lists, and loading code presets.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent explorer blurb perfectly.

edge_cases.rationale: Lacks any HTML escaping utility. Placing custom double quotes in the input yields corrupted markup due to quote collisions inside the data-data attribute on line 191. Furthermore, setting target and data directly inside innerHTML on lines 223-229 allows arbitrary HTML markup/script injections into the properties table. This directly violates the repository's escaping rules.

references.rationale: Properly cites the official ChromeStatus tracking link under see also.

design_system.rationale: Neat layout structure placing compact tables, readable gray rows, and underlines.

openQuestions.title: Add HTML escaping utility to avoid attribute and DOM injection
openQuestions.detail: Implement a complete escapeHtml helper that sanitizes '&', '<', '>', '"', and '\''. Use it when setting data-data and data-target attributes on line 191, and when writing raw text directly into the piTable innerHTML block on lines 223-229.
